data.title: Update approach-decision-maker Dimension 4 to apply complexity-gate gates
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE (RED test assertion created by Task 8307-3cd0; this task makes test_approach_decision_maker_references_complexity_gate pass)

## What to Change
In `plugins/dso/agents/approach-decision-maker.md`:

### 1. Add Read instruction with graceful degradation (after Step 1: Load Context)
```
Read `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`. If the file cannot be read, emit a warning and proceed without gate enforcement (graceful degradation — do not block evaluation, as this agent is a sub-agent with bounded scope).
```
Note: Unlike brainstorm and implementation-plan, this agent uses graceful degradation rather than a hard stop. The agent can still evaluate proposals without the gate file; it just cannot enforce structured GATE/CHECKED/FINDING/VERDICT output.

### 2. Update Dimension 4 (Simplicity) scoring table
Replace or augment the current Dimension 4 "What 4 or 5 looks like" / "What below 4 looks like" criteria to use complexity-gate gate verdicts:

| Score | Criteria |
|-------|----------|
| 5 | All applicable complexity gates PASS; proposal uses the simplest path satisfying all done definitions; no YAGNI violations; no Rule of Three violations; no unjustified library dependencies |
| 4 | One gate produces FAIL with a satisfactory justified-complexity block (evidence cited, override documented) |
| 3 | Gate verdicts present but one justified-complexity block is thin (evidence implied, not explicit) |
| 2 | Gate verdicts absent for a proposal that clearly warrants checking (adds library, new abstraction, or performance optimization) |
| 1 | Proposal fails YAGNI or Rule of Three with no justified-complexity block; complexity is purely speculative |

### 3. Require named gate in rationale output
When flagging a proposal as over-engineered (Dimension 4 score ≤ 3), the `rationale_summary` and `context` fields in the APPROACH_DECISION output must name the specific gate violated (e.g., "Proposal 1 violates Rule of Three: the proposed abstraction has 0 existing call sites"). When no gate fires, the `context` should note: "All complexity gates pass."

## What NOT to Change
- The APPROACH_DECISION signal format (mode, selected_proposal_index, context, decision, consequences, rationale_summary fields) — output contract is additive only
- Dimensions 1-3, 5 (codebase alignment, blast radius, testability, robustness) — unchanged
- The existing anti-pattern detection list (golden hammer, premature abstraction, etc.) — only augment Dimension 4 scoring guidance
- No new JSON output fields — the named gate appears within the existing `context` and `rationale_summary` string values

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plugins/dso/agents/approach-decision-maker.md` contains a Read instruction for `shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`
  Verify: `grep -q "shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md" plugins/dso/agents/approach-decision-maker.md`
- [ ] Dimension 4 scoring table references gate verdicts or named gates
  Verify: `grep -qi "gate.*PASS\|gate.*FAIL\|complexity.gate\|YAGNI\|Rule of Three" plugins/dso/agents/approach-decision-maker.md`
- [ ] Rationale output requirement for named gate is documented
  Verify: `grep -qi "named gate\|gate violated\|gate.*violated\|violated.*gate\|complexity gates pass" plugins/dso/agents/approach-decision-maker.md`
- [ ] test-complexity-gate-shared-prompt.sh test_approach_decision_maker_references_complexity_gate section passes
  Verify: `bash tests/skills/test-complexity-gate-shared-prompt.sh 2>&1 | grep -E "PASS.*approach|approach.*PASS"`
- [ ] `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci` passes
  Verify: `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci`
